rulings.Case Title: ROBERT G BAKER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS RELFECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Rpi Company'S Motion And Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiff Robert Baker To Appear And Testify At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, June 17, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant RPI Company's motion to compel plaintiff Robert Baker to appear and testify at deposition is granted. As agreed upon by the parties, the scope of the deposition is limited to plaintiff's medical condition and damages. Mr. Baker shall appear for an in-person deposition. As agreed to by defendant, such deposition shall occur at a location convenient to plaintiff. No time limit is imposed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH JOHNSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion And Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiff Kenneth Johnson To Appear And Testify At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, June 17, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant RPI Company's motion to compel plaintiff to appear and testify at deposition is continued on the court's own motion to June 24, 2014. = (503/TLJ))

rulings.Case Title: HERB ROMINE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Rheem Manufacturing Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Expert Witness, Or Alternatively, To Augment Expert Witness Pared-Down List
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, June 17, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Rheem Manufacturing Company's motion to substitute expert witness, or alternatively, to augment expert witness pared-down list is off calendar as moot pursuant to the stipulation and order which the Court has signed. = (503/TLJ)
